commit.message:
fix: add hard server-side safety net for feeder endpoint types

Even with correct normalizeType and AI prompt, if Claude returns
"Generator" for a load endpoint, nothing previously stopped it.

New sanitizeTopology() function is a HARD SAFETY NET applied to ALL
topology generation paths (image analysis, text generation, pre-chat):

Pass 1: All-transformer feeders → [CB, GenericLoad]
Pass 2: Stray single-transformer feeders → move to transformers[]
Pass 3: For EVERY feeder:
  - Normalize all element types through normalizeType()
  - If endpoint is Generator/Motor but label doesn't say generator/DG →
    force to GenericLoad
  - If endpoint is breaker/CT/Isolator → append GenericLoad

Also applied sanitizeTopology() to controller's generateSLDFromText
and preGenerationChat endpoints which previously had no safety net.

Removed duplicate stray-transformer code from generateSLDFromImage
(now handled by shared sanitizeTopology).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: audit complet + documentation du projet

README.md — réécrit pour refléter l'état réel (v0.2)
  - Table des fonctionnalités avec statut
  - Structure de fichiers à jour
  - Mention des fichiers legacy et dépendances inutilisées
  - Raccourcis clavier exacts

ARCHITECTURE.md — documentation technique complète
  - Flux de données (ASCII diagram)
  - Rôle et API de chaque module (Editor, ITool, History, Storage)
  - Système de coordonnées viewBox 800×600 + conversion getScreenCTM
  - Cycle de vie complet d'un outil
  - Rendu miniatures SVG inline
  - Liste des fichiers legacy et dépendances à nettoyer

ROADMAP.md — feuille de route par phases
  - Phase 1 ✅ (terminé)
  - Phase 2 : outils essentiels (ligne, remplissage, triangle, texte, étoile)
  - Phase 3 : qualité et interactions (sélection, export PNG, renommage)
  - Phase 4 : ergonomie avancée (zoom, grille, PWA)
  - Phase 5 : fonctionnalités avancées (calques, cloud…)
  - Table de dette technique priorisée

docs/add-a-tool.md — guide pas-à-pas pour ajouter un outil
  - Interface ITool expliquée
  - API Editor disponible
  - Conversion coordonnées obligatoire
  - Template complet commenté
  - Exemples réels (EraserTool, RectTool)
  - Bonnes pratiques + checklist de test

commit.message:
fix(config): .env.example as single source of truth for all configuration (#511)

* feat: auto-resume + HPO grid — training workflow (#365, #401)

Phase 1: Fix result.fold_count → result.n_folds bug in run_training_flow.py
  (line 112 crashed at runtime; regression test via AST scan, not regex).

Phase 2: Auto-resume integration tests with mocked MLflow — verify
  find_completed_config() and load_fold_result_from_mlflow() without a server.

Phase 3: Experiment YAML configs — configs/experiments/dynunet_grid.yaml
  (3 losses × 2 LRs × 2 batch sizes = 12 combos) and smoke_test.yaml (1 combo).

Phase 4: Implement train_all_hyperparam_combos.sh — replaces placeholder;
  reads YAML via yaml.safe_load (Rule #16), launches via docker compose (Rule #17),
  supports --dry-run / --config / --families / --losses flags.

Phase 5: Fix run_hpo.py placeholder — raises NotImplementedError with clear
  instructions to use Prefect hpo-flow deployment instead of returning 0.0.

32 new tests, all passing. Closes #365, #401.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): .env.example as single source of truth — eliminate scattered config (Rule #22)

Problem: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI and other service URLs were defined in 29+ places:
Dockerfiles, docker-compose files, Dynaconf TOMLs, Python flow files, SkyPilot
YAMLs, and Prefect work-pools — all with slightly different hardcoded values.
This caused the mlruns vs mlruns-docker split seen in practice.

Changes:
- .env.example: expanded to complete config contract — all URLs, ports,
  credentials, Docker-internal hostnames, per-service DB names, BentoML home.
  Every variable used anywhere in the project is defined here.
- docker-compose.flows.yml: x-common-env now uses ${MLFLOW_DOCKER_HOST:-...}
  substitution for MLflow, Prefect, MinIO — no more hardcoded service URLs.
- docker-compose.yml: MLflow --port, DB names, MinIO creds, Langfuse DB,
  Prefect DB, BentoML home — all parameterized from .env variables.
- Dockerfile.train + Dockerfile.biostatistics: removed ENV MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI.
  Dockerfiles must not hardcode service URLs; compose injects them at runtime.
- configs/deployment/{settings,development,staging,production}.toml: removed
  mlflow_tracking_uri — consumed directly from env var by resolve_tracking_uri().
- work-pools.yaml + skypilot YAMLs: variable substitution for all URIs.
- 8 Prefect flow files + flow_contract.py: replace os.environ.get("MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI",
  "mlruns") with resolve_tracking_uri() — single authoritative resolution path.
- .gitignore: add mlruns-docker/ (stale Docker bind-mount artifact).
- CLAUDE.md: add Rule #22 — .env.example single-source contract with banned
  patterns and correct patterns. Added to "What AI Must NEVER Do" list.
- tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py: 12 AST-based enforcement tests that
  fail immediately if anyone violates the single-source invariant.

Zero new test failures (3 pre-existing failures unchanged: #510, annotation_flow,
config_composition).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Fix 'cannot enter context' error in Guardian ThreadPoolExecutor

Remove manual copy_context().run() — ThreadPoolExecutor already propagates
context to worker threads in Python 3.7+, and re-entering the same context
inside LangGraph caused a RuntimeError.

Flatten module structure for Temper nightly compatibility

- Add config.temper.md to define "orm" library (required by Temper)
- Move sql/ files into src/ (sql_builder, sql_model, sql_imports, sql_tests)
  to avoid cross-module @I stage parallelism failures
- Move test files from test/ into src/ for same-module visibility
- Remove intra-module import statements (same-module files share scope)
- Convert when expressions to if-else chains (nightly parser compat)
- Fix nullable Int32? narrowing with local variable pattern
- Rename test helper sid->csid in changeset_test to avoid name collision

All 51 tests pass locally with temper test -b js.

feat: add plan-based automation limits, tenant isolation, typed Inngest events, and meetings UI enhancements

- Add centralized plan tier limits (lib/plan-limits.ts) with per-category automation caps
- Enforce tenant isolation at Prisma layer (lib/prisma.ts) with companyId scoping on updateMany/deleteMany
- Add atomic category limit checking (lib/automation-limit-check.ts) with Serializable transactions
- Add automation downgrade logic (lib/automation-downgrade.ts) to deactivate excess rules on plan change
- Add typed Inngest event schemas (lib/inngest/events.ts) for type-safe event dispatching
- Refactor cache-service signatures for consistency
- Enhance meetings UI with background decor, status badges, and calendar improvements
- Update validation rules for dashboard widgets and automation inputs
- Fix tenant isolation gap in automation-downgrade updateMany
- Fix React Compiler memoization error in MiniMeetingsWidget
- Update tests to match new tenant isolation and plan-limit APIs

to the jobs page. Rebrand all user-facing "scraping" text to

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

UX opravy: 10 důležitých (D1-D13) + 7 kritických (K1-K7)

DŮLEŽITÉ:
- D1: Import wizard stepper (4 kroky: nahrání → mapování → náhled → výsledek)
- D2: Nápověda pod mapovacími selecty při importu hlasování
- D3: Badge "Hlasováno: X/Y bodů" na detailu lístku
- D4: Hromadný reset zpracovaných lístků s checkboxy
- D7: Badge "Odesílá se" a "Pozastaveno" v rozesílce
- D9: Kompaktní filtr kontaktů (4 segmenty v jedné bublině)
- D10: Flash varování při neplatném emailu vlastníka
- D11: Červené zvýraznění pole při chybě validace jednotky
- D13: Flash zpráva po vytvoření vlastníka

KRITICKÉ:
- K1: Zobrazení počtu neúplně hlasovaných lístků v kvórum sekci

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix blank map: use position:absolute;inset:0 instead of height:100%

height:100% on a flex-grown parent doesn't reliably resolve in all
browsers — Leaflet read 0px height and rendered an empty canvas.
Switching to position:absolute;inset:0 fills .map-wrap via its
position:relative containing block, independent of how flex resolves
the height.

chore: #18 Kubernetes integration tests in CI with testcontainers k3s (#31)

* chore: #18 add self-contained Kubernetes integration tests via testcontainers k3s

- Add integration_test.go with //go:build integration_k8s tag; spins up a
  k3s cluster per test run using testcontainers, deploys randomlog from the
  public GHCR image, and verifies KubernetesSource streams valid JSON logs
- Add kubernetes-integration CI job: checkout, setup-go, go test — no KIND,
  no kubectl, no image builds required
- Add testcontainers-go/modules/k3s v0.40.0 as a direct dependency
- Wire kubernetes-integration coverage into SonarCloud merge step

* added vscode to gitignore

* test: #18 split k8s integration tests into helpers + focused tests

- Move cluster startup, kubeconfig wiring, deploy, and pod-ready poll
  into helpers_test.go with TestMain so all tests share one k3s instance
- Rewrite integration_test.go with three focused tests:
  TestIntegrationKubernetesSource (log streaming + JSON validation),
  TestIntegrationKubernetesSourceStop (idempotent Stop),
  TestIntegrationKubernetesSourceLogsChannel (channel not nil)
- Fix SQ issue: check error from kubeconfigFile.Close()
- Fix goroutine leak: replace time.After in poll loop with time.NewTicker
- Fix goroutine leak in source.go watchPods: replace time.After with timer
- Log re-list error on 410 Gone recovery instead of silently swallowing
- Log rc.Close() error in streamContainerLogs for connection-level failures

* test: #18 fix timer goroutine leaks in source_test.go and clarify TailLines comment

- Replace time.After with time.NewTimer+Stop in waitFor helper and
  the Deleted-event assertion to prevent goroutine accumulation
- Clarify TailLines=0 comment to match Kubernetes API semantics

commit.message:
Add ai-employee as general-purpose fallback role (#2)

## Summary
- Add `ai-employee` role — a general-purpose worker that loads all
installed skills via `"*"` wildcard and executes delegated tasks
- Update `ai-ceo` to delegate to `ai-employee` as a last resort instead
of doing work itself, with explicit priority order: specialized role >
ai-employee > ask the user
- Add `ai-employee` to the README roles table

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify `ai-employee` loads all installed skills via `"*"` wildcard
- [ ] Confirm `ai-ceo` delegates to `ai-employee` when no specialized
role matches
- [ ] Validate `ai-ceo` still prefers specialized roles over
`ai-employee`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

fix: read stream-json from stderr, add timeout + strip CLAUDECODE env

claude --print --output-format stream-json --verbose sends events to
stderr, not stdout. investigate/enrich were reading stdout → 0 events.

- Extract shared spawn-claude helper that reads stderr for events
- Add 5-minute process timeout (SIGTERM → SIGKILL) to prevent blocking
- Strip CLAUDECODE from child env to avoid nested-session rejection
- Share worktree across investigate → enrich → execute phases
- Add worktree_path column to tasks table (migration 018)
- Support existingWorktree in SessionManager.createSession

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Batch weight processing in GEMV compute, device rmsnorm cleanup (~78ms/tok, 12.8 tok/s)

Process weight tiles in BLOCK-sized batches in compute kernel to reduce
cb_wait/cb_pop synchronization overhead. BLOCK compile-time arg matches
reader's TRID pipelining granularity. Also cleaned up failed device rmsnorm
attempts (keeping host rmsnorm which is faster due to AVX-512 vs RISC-V no FPU).

commit.message:
v10.1.2176: systemic pipeline fix — material fallback, MISSING_CONTEXT contract, semantic keys, stale clarification

TS-FIX-SYSTEMIC-PIPELINE-MATERIAL-CLARIFICATION-V1-01:

Issue 1: Unresolved materials no longer block RECORD_ACTIVITY. Raw material
text is preserved (material_id=NULL, material_name=raw text). Activity is
always recorded.

Issue 2: Unified missing_context_result() contract with entity_type,
raw_name, data.missing_fields, and semantic_outcome.

Issue 3: Added activity_semantic_meaning and dose_rate_basis to KNOWN_KEYS
in strict_normalizer so LLM semantic hints survive normalization.

Issue 4: Stale Redis clarifications now cleared on same-domain action_type
mismatch (e.g., ADD_FIELD→UPDATE_FIELD_NAME), not only cross-domain.
Short clarification answers (<40 chars, single token) are preserved.

feat(audit): context-aware v1.1 audit engine with severity classification

- queries.ts: all React Query hooks + TypeScript types
- Client home: real stats, sessions, PRs, checkin banner with navigation
- Client workout: full workout logger with skip/move day options
- Client account: profile name edit + password change + sign out
- Client checkins: view pending/completed check-ins, submit answers
- Trainer dashboard: live stats grid, sessions list, clients preview
- Trainer clients: Stack nav (list + detail), searchable with status filter
- Trainer client detail: stats, approve/reject pending, overrides, sessions
- Trainer settings: profile name edit + password change + sign out

Backend:
- Updated 6 API routes to use getAuthUser() for Bearer JWT support:
  settings/profile, settings/password, checkins, checkins/[id]/submit,
  clients/[id]/approve, clients/[id]/reject
- New: GET /api/mobile/client-detail/[id] with stats, program, sessions, overrides

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: fittrack-app
repository.description: FitTrack - Smart gym and personal trainer management app. Built with Next.js 16, Prisma, Supabase, NextAuth.js.

commit.message:
feat: spring-tension drag — nearby nodes pulled more than distant ones

Replace rigid-body drag with BFS falloff:
- hop 1 neighbours move at 40% of drag delta
- hop 2 at 16%, hop 3 at 6.4% (TENSION^depth)
- Max depth 3 hops; nodes beyond that are unaffected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
